summary: Matched the grshrine stage module by replacing asm/melee/gr/grshrine.s with src/melee/gr/grshrine.c and a local header, updating object lists to build the C object, and generalizing a duplicated stage data struct into StageData in stage.h. The PR has no body or review comments, so intent is inferred from the diff: it was primarily an assembly-to-C decompilation/matching conversion for the shrine stage.
what_changed: Deleted the 351-line assembly file asm/melee/gr/grshrine.s and added a 190-line C implementation in src/melee/gr/grshrine.c.
Added src/melee/gr/grshrine.h containing static prototypes for grshrine-local functions and callbacks.
Switched obj_files.mk from building asm/melee/gr/grshrine.s.o to src/melee/gr/grshrine.c.o.
Added src/melee/gr/grshrine.c.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk, indicating this source needed unscheduled epilogue handling for matching.
Added a shared StageData typedef to src/melee/gr/stage.h, with fields for flags, StageCallbacks pointer, data filename, lifecycle/collision callbacks, and unused padding.
Updated src/melee/gr/grstory.c to use StageData lbl_803E274C instead of repeating an anonymous struct with the same layout.
Added a typed declaration for func_80011A50 in include/functions.h: void func_80011A50(Vec*, s32, f32, f32, f32, f32, f32, f32, f32).
Recreated grshrine static data in C, including Vec3 lbl_803B8298 = { 0.5f, 0.0f, 0.0f }, StageCallbacks lbl_803E50E8[3], global StageData lbl_803E5130, and static sbss pointer lbl_804D6A18.
Implemented stage startup, teardown, object creation, per-map callbacks, flag bitfield writes, and a height comparison callback corresponding to labels from the deleted assembly.
smart_moves: Factored the repeated stage data layout into StageData in stage.h rather than keeping grshrine and grstory with separate anonymous structs.
Modeled the data table as StageCallbacks lbl_803E50E8[3], preserving the assembly table structure and making func_80201D20 index directly into callback records.
Used typed Map fields such as map_id, x1C_callback, x10_flags.b5, and x11_flags.b012 to express byte/bit operations from assembly in readable C.
Preserved exact label-style names for unknown/static callbacks, avoiding speculative semantic names while still enabling a full C match.
Kept grshrine functions static where local to the translation unit, matching the original module-local nature of many labels.
Matched float constants through source expressions where practical, including M_PI / 3 for the 0x3F860A92 value and explicit camera/position bounds passed to func_80011A50.
Included unused stack locals in some functions, such as unused arrays in func_80201C64 and lbl_80201E9C, to satisfy compiler code generation requirements.
decomp_lessons: When matching stage modules, first identify common table layouts; local anonymous structs can often become a reusable StageData typedef.

Stage object construction code commonly follows the pattern func_801C14D0(id), initialize Map fields, GObj_SetupGXLink, optionally copy callback3 into map->x1C_callback, call callback0, then register callback2 with func_8038FD54.
Do not over-name unknown callback labels prematurely. Address labels like lbl_80201E08 can be retained when semantics are still unclear.
For table-driven stage code, matching depends on preserving callback table order and nullable entries exactly.
Small bitfield writes from rlwimi/stb instructions may be expressible through existing Map flag bitfields, e.g. map->x11_flags.b012 = 2 and map->x10_flags.b5 = 1.
Build-list changes are part of the decompilation proof: remove the asm object from TEXT_O_FILES and add the C object, including any epilogue scheduling workaround list if required.
Promoting a helper prototype in include/functions.h can remove guesswork from matched stage code and document the argument order observed from assembly.
naming_conventions: The module follows the gr<stage> naming pattern: grshrine.c and grshrine.h for the Shrine stage.
Unidentified functions and data keep address-derived names such as func_80201C64, func_80201D20, lbl_803E50E8, and lbl_803E5130.
Shared stage structs use PascalCase typedef names, shown by StageCallbacks and newly added StageData.
Header guard style in the added file is lowercase with underscores: _grshrine_h_.
The stage data filename is embedded as a short DAT path string, here "/GrSh.dat".
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a full assembly translation unit with C while preserving object order through obj_files.mk.
Used EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED for src/melee/gr/grshrine.c.o, implying an epilogue scheduling mismatch workaround was necessary.
Translated the assembly rodata vector into a static const Vec3 to match data placement and values.
Translated assembly callback tables into typed C arrays and structs rather than raw .4byte data.
Used a local M_PI define to reproduce the float constant passed as the third angular argument to func_80011A50.
Preserved the OSReport failure path in func_80201D20 with the original message text, source filename "grshrine.c", line value 205, and object id argument.
Implemented the final callback lbl_80201F4C as a y-coordinate comparison after func_8000B1CC fills a Vec3 from a joint pointer.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename grshrine lbl_80201xxx callbacks to semantic stage callback names.
Search for other stage modules using StageData after this PR to confirm the shared layout.
Search for EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED gr stage objects to identify recurring matching constraints.
Compare grshrine.c with grstory.c and other gr*.c modules for reusable stage object creation patterns.
Look for later changes to func_80011A50 prototype or naming.
